data.content: No version conflicts — all packages are unique across the three projects. I'll create `Directory.Packages.props` and strip versions from the `.csproj` files.

arguments.file_text:
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.AspNetCore.OpenApi" Version="8.0.24" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.12.0" />

data.reasoningText:
Now I have all the information I need. Let me convert to Central Package Management:

1. Create `Directory.Packages.props` at the solution root with all unique packages and the highest version for any conflicts.
2. Remove `Version` attributes from all `PackageReference` elements in the `.csproj` files.

Packages across all projects:
- Core: System.Text.Json 9.0.0
- Tests: Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk 17.12.0, xunit 2.9.3, xunit.runner.visualstudio 2.8.2 (PrivateAssets="all")


- Web: OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting 1.15.0, Microsoft.AspNetCore.OpenApi 8.0.24

Since there are no version conflicts across projects, I'll create the Directory.Packages.props file with all packages and update each csproj to remove the version attributes while preserving any PrivateAssets metadata.

    <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>

    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.12.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting" Version="1.15.0" />

    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.9.3" />

  </ItemGroup>
</Project>

data.content:
Done. Here's what I did:

**Created** `simple-solution/Directory.Packages.props` with `ManagePackageVersionsCentrally` enabled and all 6 packages:

| Package | Version |
|---|---|
| Microsoft.AspNetCore.OpenApi | 8.0.24 |
| Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk | 17.12.0 |
| OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting | 1.15.0 |
| System.Text.Json | 9.0.0 |
| xunit | 2.9.3 |
| xunit.runner.visualstudio | 2.8.2 |

**Updated** `Core.csproj`, `Tests.csproj`, and `Web.csproj` — removed `Version` attributes from all `PackageReference` elements while preserving other attributes like `PrivateAssets`.

No version conflicts were found (all packages were unique across projects).
